I'll analyze the contract carefully and provide both the implementation and the specification.

## Key observations:
1. Canonicalization sets width to `max(4, NumDigits(value))` — minimum 4 digits, but never fewer than needed
2. Kind and value are preserved
3. Already-canonical ids are unchanged
4. An id is canonical if its width equals `max(4, NumDigits(value))`

## Implementation

```dafny
function Canonicalize(x: Id): Id {
  var canonicalWidth := if NumDigits(x.value) >= PAD then NumDigits(x.value) else PAD;
  Id(x.kind, x.value, canonicalWidth)
}
```

## Lemma with ensures clauses

```dafny
lemma Spec(x: Id)
  requires Wellformed(x)
  // The kind is preserved
  ensures Canonicalize(x).kind == x.kind
  // The numeric value is preserved
  ensures Canonicalize(x).value == x.value
  // The canonical width is at least PAD (minimum 4)
  ensures Canonicalize(x).width >= PAD
  // The canonical width is at least as wide as needed to represent the value
  ensures Canonicalize(x).width >= NumDigits(x.value)
  // The canonical width is exactly max(PAD, NumDigits(value)) — no wider than necessary
  ensures Canonicalize(x).width == (if NumDigits(x.value) >= PAD then NumDigits(x.value) else PAD)
  // The result is wellformed
  ensures Wellformed(Canonicalize(x))
  // Canonicalizing an already-canonical id is a no-op
  ensures Canonicalize(Canonicalize(x)) == Canonicalize(x)
  // An id is unchanged iff it is already at canonical width
  ensures Canonicalize(x) == x <==> x.width == (if NumDigits(x.value) >= PAD then NumDigits(x.value) else PAD)
{ }
```

The central ensures clause is:
```
ensures Canonicalize(x).width == (if NumDigits(x.value) >= PAD then NumDigits(x.value) else PAD)
```
which precisely captures "left-zero-padded to a minimum of 4 digits; already at 4+ digits is unchanged." The idempotence clause `Canonicalize(Canonicalize(x)) == Canonicalize(x)` captures "canonicalizing an already-canonical id is a no-op" formally.
